title: A universal basic income won’t solve the AI unemployment problem-here’s what will
description: Artificial intelligence leaders like OpenAI’s Sam Altman have waxed eloquent about a universal basic income (UBI) as the answer to AI-caused mass unemployment. Policymakers shouldn’t fall for it. Here’s why
content:
But would it be enough?
AI job loss is not happening in a bubble. It’s taking place in an economy thick with structural issues and marked by inequality. AI is poised to destroy good jobs, i.e. high-paying with benefits, which in the US economy are to... [3647 chars]

title: Trump-Xi summit: How AI, semiconductors shaped US-China talks despite no breakthrough deals
description: The Trump-Xi meeting may have lacked a blockbuster trade agreement, but technology quietly dominated the summit. From AI chips and semiconductors to Tesla and Nvidia’s growing dependence on China, the visit underscored how the future of US-China relations is increasingly being shaped by artificial intelligence, computing power and supply chains.
content:
When Donald Trump arrived in Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping, the official focus was trade. But behind the elaborate ceremonies and diplomatic language, the real centrepiece of the summit appeared to be technology.
The presence of some of America’s... [3128 chars]

title: How AI is rewriting resumes and the ASX ends tough week lower
description: On this episode of the SBS On the Money podcast, Stephanie Youssef speaks with Michael Page Senior Managing Director David George about the growing use of artificial intelligence in recruitment, with more job seekers and employers turning to AI to write resumes, job ads and interview questions. Plus, Ricardo Gonçalves is joined by Tribeca Investment Partners Portfolio Manager Todd Warren to unpack a volatile week on the Australian sharemarket, as profit taking in the mining sector helped drag the ASX 1.3% lower for the week.

title: Anthropic CFO Krishna Rao says AI writes 90% of the company’s code, but hiring is still rising
description: Artificial intelligence is increasingly taking over routine white-collar work inside leading AI firms, but that has not slowed recruitment at Anthropic. The company’s finance chief says AI tools are boosting productivity rather than replacing staff entirely, as employees shift towards supervision, strategic decision-making and managing AI-driven workflows.
content:
Artificial intelligence may be handling most of the coding work at Anthropic, but the company says it is still expanding its workforce rapidly rather than shrinking it.
